title: System Recovery Time Objective Tracking from DR Drills

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 CP-10 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CP-10 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.80: System Recovery Time Objective Tracking from DR Drills.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.80' running on index=grc sourcetype=dr:drill or snow:change_request DR template, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=grc sourcetype=dr:drill earliest=-365d
| eval rto_min=recovery_end_epoch - failover_start_epoch
| lookup app_tiers.csv application OUTPUT rto_target_min
| where rto_min > rto_target_min
| table drill_id, application, rto_min, rto_target_min, notes
description: Captures measured recovery time metrics from DR drills for CP-10 system recovery and reconstitution.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes dr:drill, dr:drill`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ensure epoch fields populated at drill completion; (2) store raw drill packets in secure share; (3) feed POA&M if missed; (4) integrate with IR-8 updates. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=dr:drill`, `sourcetype=dr:drill``; Lookups: `app_tiers`, `app_tiers.csv`.
visualization: Table (RTO breaches), Bar chart (by app), Single value (worst RTO). Key fields: `application`, `drill_id`, `notes`, `rto_min`, `rto_target_min`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=dr:drill` or `snow:change_request` DR template.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure epoch fields populated at drill completion; (2) store raw drill packets in secure share; (3) feed POA&M if missed; (4) integrate with IR-8 updates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=dr:drill earliest=-365d
| eval rto_min=recovery_end_epoch - failover_start_epoch
| lookup app_tiers.csv application OUTPUT rto_target_min
| where rto_min > rto_target_min
| table drill_id, application, rto_min, rto_target_min, notes
```

Understanding this SPL

**System Recovery Time Objective Tracking from DR Drills (CP-10)** — Captures measured recovery time metrics from DR drills for CP-10 system recovery and reconstitution.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=dr:drill` or `snow:change_request` DR template. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: dr:drill. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=dr:drill, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rto_min** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where rto_min > rto_target_min` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: application, drill_id, notes, rto_min, rto_target_min.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (RTO breaches), Bar chart (by app), Single value (worst RTO)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for captures measured recovery time metrics from dr drills for system recovery and reconstitution. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
